title: Where Signals Cross and Compare

features.name: the-archive-antenna
features.desc-on-touch: Two antennae touching at their tips, broadcasting on the same frequency but not in phase. They're comparing notes. Sometimes they align and something new propagates; sometimes they drift and become two separate conversations.

features.name: the-fence-reading
features.desc-on-touch: Fence posts arranged in rows, each holding a different archive's signal in its wood grain. You can read them if you know which way the grain runs in your own country.

features.name: the-passage-logic
features.desc-on-touch: A circuit made of signal and space, looped back on itself. Stories from elsewhere travel through it and arrive as stories from here, slightly changed by the crossing.

features.name: the-comparison-pool
features.desc-on-touch: Water that reflects two different skies depending on which archive is reading it. Today they're almost the same colour. Tomorrow they might not be.

inscription: where one archive learns what another archive keeps; comparison is its own kind of reading

file.name: Cross_Archive_Signal_Log_Renewal_Variant.wav
file.note: A recording of two broadcasts overlapping. Someone singing about renewal and return. The pitch matches a frequency the soil remembers, documented nowhere yet.

exits.label: back to the cloud-architecture where the archives first began comparing what they see
further across toward where the other archive is learning to broadcast back
upward toward the horizon where more archives are waking up to read the same sky
